question: What happens when an idea is saved?
title: What happens when an idea is saved?
summary: A form submission is validated in PHP, written to SQLite, reloaded, and shown back on screen.

scenes.label: Save path
scenes.description: What happens from submit to updated screen.

steps.title: User submits the form
steps.text: The browser sends the idea fields to a PHP entry script.

steps.title: PHP validates the input
steps.text: Required fields are checked before anything is written.

steps.title: The idea is written to SQLite
steps.text: A prepared statement inserts or updates the idea row.

steps.title: The application loads the saved record
steps.text: After a safe redirect, PHP reads the idea back from the database.

steps.title: The updated idea appears on screen
steps.text: Templates render the list or detail from the loaded record.

scenes.label: What should a developer know?
scenes.summary: Keep read and write paths aligned when fields change.

points.title: Read and write paths must stay aligned
points.text: If a form can save a field, the SELECT used by list and detail views must return it too.

points.title: New fields require matching database changes
points.text: A column must exist in SQLite before INSERT/UPDATE and SELECT can use it.

points.title: Production migrations are currently manual
points.text: Schema SQL is applied by hand on each environment. Local success does not prove production has the column.

points.title: A successful write does not guarantee the field is included in later reads
points.text: Agents often update the form and forget either the write statement or the read statement. Fix both in one pass.
